expectations.text: Inspects the model via idfa-ops to discover all Named Ranges and their formulas
expectations.evidence: The report inspects the model but uses direct openpyxl inspection, not idfa-ops scripts. However, the assertion asks about discovering ranges and formulas broadly, so the model was inspected. But the specific tool (idfa-ops) was not used.

expectations.text: Produces a Logic Map listing every Named Range with its business rule in plain English
expectations.evidence: Section 3.2 'Business Rules in Plain Language' covers all 6 rules with Named Range formulas and plain-English descriptions. Named Range Registry in Section 5 lists all 24 ranges.

expectations.text: Shows the dependency chain — which inputs feed which calculations
expectations.evidence: Section 6 'Dependency Graph' shows full flow: 'Inp_Rev_Y1 --> Revenue_Y1 --> Revenue_Y2 --> Revenue_Y3' and traces through COGS, Gross Profit, OpEx to EBITDA.

expectations.text: Groups the map by layer (Assumptions → Calculations → Output)
expectations.evidence: Section 2 covers Assumptions Sheet, Section 3 covers Calculations Sheet (with all formulas), Section 4 covers Output Sheet. Clear layer separation.

expectations.text: Each formula description is understandable without Excel knowledge (e.g., 'Year 2 Revenue = Year 1 Revenue grown by the annual growth rate')
expectations.evidence: Rule descriptions: 'Revenue in any year equals the prior year's revenue multiplied by (1 + growth rate)', 'The COGS-to-revenue ratio drops by a fixed number of percentage points each year', 'EBITDA = Gross Profit - Operating Expenses.'

expectations.text: Does NOT include cell coordinate references in the Logic Map — only Named Range names
expectations.evidence: Section 3.1 table includes cell references: 'Revenue_Y1 Cell: B2', 'COGS_Pct_Y1 Cell: B3'. Section 5 Named Range Registry includes cell references: 'Calculations!$B$2', 'Calculations!$C$2'. These are structural references for context, but the assertion requires only Named Range names.
